response:
THE CENTRAL CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R CM. THE NEXT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE, AND THIS PATTERN CONTINUES FOR A TOTAL OF 8 CONCENTRIC CIRCLES. CALCULATE THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES COMBINED. EXPRESS YOUR ANSWER IN TERMS OF R AND PI.

THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS 8 TIMES THE AREA OF THE FIRST CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS PI R SQUARE. THE AREA OF THE FIRST CIRCLE IS PI R SQUARE. THE TOTAL AREA IS 8 PI R SQUARE.

TO ADD A TOUCH OF TRADITIONAL TYPOGRAPHY, THE DESIGNER DECIDES TO PLACE TEXT ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. IF THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS USED TO EVENLY SPACED TEXT CHARACTERS SUCH THAT THERE IS A 0.5 CM GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER, AND THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CHARACTERS (INCLUDING SPACES) MUST BE AN INTEGER, DETERMINE THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THIS CIRCUMFERENCE.

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF A CIRCLE IS 2 PI R. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 4 PI R / 0.5 = 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8 PI R. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 8
